title: Add Classification Attribute to Metrics Service 2 Request and Response
description:
Update the Service 2 request to support a Classification attribute that distinguishes between confirmed metrics and new widgets requiring AI analysis.

### Request Classifications

- **Metric** — An existing metric from the DataTools metrics dictionary that has already been confirmed. Sent to the AI service so it can identify duplicates and overlaps against new dashboard widgets.
- **Analyze** — A new dashboard widget (the current behavior). Tells the AI to review and generate a recommendation.

### Response Classifications

Service 2 will return one of three classification types for each analyzed widget:

1. **Recommendation** — A genuinely new metric suggestion. Displayed in the Recommendations tab as it does today.
2. **Merge** — The AI found an existing metric with a slightly different name. Surfaced as a merge suggestion so the user can reconcile the naming.
3. **Duplicate** — The AI matched the widget to an existing dictionary metric. Automatically routed to the Ignore section so the user does not need to manually dismiss it.

The JSON payload was restructured into two arrays (`metric` and `analyze`) with the existing metrics dictionary appended to the request body alongside the dashboard widgets.
acceptance_criteria: Each widget sent to Service 2 includes a Classification field set to 'Analyze'
Each existing dictionary metric sent includes a Classification field set to 'Metric'
The JSON payload contains separate 'metric' and 'analyze' arrays
Existing metrics include their categories (Line of Business, Topic) and aliases
Response items classified as 'Recommendation' appear in the Recommendations tab
Response items classified as 'Merge' are surfaced as merge suggestions
Response items classified as 'Duplicate' are automatically moved to the Ignore section
The restructured payload passes validation on the AI service endpoint
middleware_changes: Restructured the Service 2 request builder to split the payload into 'metric' and 'analyze' arrays. Existing dictionary metrics are fetched, mapped to the shared JSON structure, and appended with Classification='Metric'. Dashboard widgets retain Classification='Analyze'. Response handler routes items by classification type.
api_changes: Service 2 request body restructured from a flat array to an object with 'metric' and 'analyze' arrays. Each item includes a new 'Classification' field. Response now returns three classification types: Recommendation, Merge, and Duplicate.
implementation_notes: The JSON structure was validated on staging by the developer before being sent to the external AI service team (Anton/Raj). The code was ready but blocked from merging until the AI service was updated to accept the new payload format.
comment_summary: Developer posted the revised JSON payload on staging for review. The format was approved. Deployment was blocked pending the external AI service update by Anton and Raj. Ticket was moved back to To Do as a proxy for 'blocked' status.
